job.name: recent-failures
job.description: Summarize recent healthcheck + other job failures in the Irrigation project. Run this when you come back after a few days away to see what happened — how often the app went unhealthy, why, and whether self-healing restarts handled it. Defaults to the last 7 days; override with the `days` option.

command.script:
#!/bin/sh # recent-failures — quick status readout for the Irrigation project. # Runs on the Rundeck server node and talks to the local API via `rd` # (for queries) and curl (for per-execution log output, which `rd # executions follow` replaces with a failure banner on failed runs). set -u
export RD_URL=http://localhost:4440 export RD_USER=admin export RD_PASSWORD=admin
DAYS="${RD_OPTION_DAYS:-7}" WINDOW="${DAYS}d"
# Establish an API session once so per-execution log fetches are cheap. COOKIE=$(mktemp) trap 'rm -f "$COOKIE"' EXIT curl -s -c "$COOKIE" -d "j_username=${RD_USER}" -d "j_password=${RD_PASSWORD}" \ "${RD_URL}/j_security_check" -o /dev/null
fetch_log() { # First N lines of an execution's output — that's where the healthcheck # printed its reason + action before exiting non-zero. curl -s -b "$COOKIE" "${RD_URL}/api/45/execution/$1/output?format=text" \ | grep -v '^Failed: ' \ | grep -v '^Execution failed: ' \ | head -n 4 }
RD_QUERY="rd executions query --noninteractive"
echo "=== Irrigation health report — last ${DAYS} day(s) ===" echo
# --- Healthcheck summary --------------------------------------------------- # Every red healthcheck means the probe found the app unhealthy (and either # restarted it or was blocked by cooldown/cap). Every green means healthy. total=$($RD_QUERY -p Irrigation -j ops/healthcheck -d "$WINDOW" -m 500 -% '%id' 2>/dev/null | grep -cE '^[0-9]+' || echo 0) failed=$($RD_QUERY -p Irrigation -j ops/healthcheck -d "$WINDOW" -s failed -m 500 -% '%id' 2>/dev/null | grep -cE '^[0-9]+' || echo 0)
if [ "$total" -eq 0 ]; then echo "Healthcheck: no runs in window (scheduler disabled? container down?)" else healthy=$((total - failed)) pct=$((healthy * 100 / total)) echo "Healthcheck: ${total} runs, ${healthy} green, ${failed} red (${pct}% healthy)" fi echo
# --- Failed healthcheck details ------------------------------------------- if [ "$failed" -gt 0 ]; then echo "--- Failed healthchecks (most recent first) ---" $RD_QUERY -p Irrigation -j ops/healthcheck -d "$WINDOW" -s failed -m 500 -% '%id|%dateStarted' 2>/dev/null \ | while IFS='|' read -r id started; do [ -n "$id" ] || continue echo " #${id} ${started}" fetch_log "$id" | sed 's/^/ /' done echo fi
# --- Other failing jobs in the project ------------------------------------ # Exclude healthcheck (already covered) and this job itself so a failure here # doesn't recurse into the summary. echo "--- Other failed jobs in window ---" other=$($RD_QUERY -p Irrigation -s failed -d "$WINDOW" -m 500 \ -X ops/healthcheck -X ops/recent-failures \ -% '%id|%dateStarted|%job.name' 2>/dev/null) if [ -z "$other" ]; then echo " (none)" else echo "$other" | while IFS='|' read -r id started job; do [ -n "$id" ] || continue echo " #${id} ${started} ${job}" done fi echo
echo "=== end of report ==="
